claims.title: Wholesale effort and thoroughness trump half measures
claims.proposition: Acquiring fortune → requires → doing business thoroughly with all might
claims.source_ref: Section: Whatever You Do, Do It With All Your Might

claims.title: Dishonesty inevitably discovered destroys opportunity
claims.proposition: Dishonesty → eventual discovery → loss of opportunity → closed paths to success
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Preserve Your Integrity

claims.title: Debt compounds against the debtor
claims.proposition: Creditors → possess → perfect memories; Money in debt → works against → the debtor while the creditor sleeps
claims.source_ref: Section: Avoid Debt, discussion of lazy apprentice Horatio; on money as servant or master

claims.title: Intoxication prevents sound judgment
claims.proposition: Intoxicating drinks → muddle the brain → making successful business impossible
claims.source_ref: Chunk 01, paragraphs on intoxicating drinks

claims.title: Politeness retains customer relationships
claims.proposition: Politeness and kindness → customer retention → profits exceed those from rudeness
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Be Polite and Kind to Your Customers

claims.title: Excellence in one's field cannot fail to succeed
claims.proposition: Excelling in profession → good habits + integrity → abundant patronage guaranteed
claims.source_ref: Chapter 4: Don't Get Above Your Business (profession overcrowding)

claims.title: Income must exceed expenditure
claims.proposition: Accumulating wealth → requires → making income exceed expenditure consistently
claims.source_ref: Introduction, foundational principle

claims.title: Retain and develop talented employees
claims.proposition: Experienced employees → increase in value → should not be readily replaced
claims.source_ref: Chapter 3: Use the Best Tools

claims.title: Cascading purchases expand lifestyle permanently
claims.proposition: A single purchase → triggers → coordinating purchases and permanent lifestyle expansion
claims.source_ref: Introduction, anecdote of the sofa; paragraph on easy come easy go

claims.title: Integrity is more valuable than wealth
claims.proposition: Integrity → foundation of success → more valuable than diamonds or money
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Preserve Your Integrity

claims.title: Choose a vocation suited to aptitude and excel
claims.proposition: Success in occupation → requires → matching natural aptitude to vocation; excellence in profession → guarantees → abundant patronage
claims.source_ref: Section: Don't Mistake Your Vocation; Chapter 4: Don't Get Above Your Business

claims.title: Speculation outside expertise destroys wealth
claims.proposition: Unfamiliar business speculation → losses despite prior success → financial ruin
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Beware of Outside Operations

claims.title: Endorsements without security enable ruin
claims.proposition: Unsecured endorsements → habitual borrowing → speculation spiral → mutual ruin
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Don't Indorse Without Security

claims.title: Starting with borrowed capital prevents real wealth building
claims.proposition: Borrowed capital → easy money → lack of value understanding → business failure
claims.source_ref: Chapter 4: Don't Get Above Your Business

claims.title: Quality products can succeed with advertising
claims.proposition: Genuine article + repetitive advertising → public knowledge → profitable sales
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Advertise Your Business

claims.title: Debt is enslavement
claims.proposition: Running into debt → creates → slavish position eroding self-respect
claims.source_ref: Section: Avoid Debt

claims.title: Economy is not about frugal behavior
claims.proposition: True economy → is not → petty cost-cutting or saving cheese-parings
claims.source_ref: Introduction, paragraph on misapprehended economy

claims.title: Good health is foundational to success
claims.proposition: Making money → requires → good health as substratum
claims.source_ref: Introduction, paragraph beginning 'The foundation of success'

claims.title: Brain and judgment matter more than mere labor
claims.proposition: Thinking workers → greater value → better employees than non-thinking hands
claims.source_ref: Chapter 3: Use the Best Tools (Beecher illustration)

claims.title: Choose a vocation suited to aptitude
claims.proposition: Success in occupation → requires → matching natural aptitude to vocation choice
claims.source_ref: Section: Don't Mistake Your Vocation

claims.title: Retain talented employees over time
claims.proposition: Experienced employees → increase in value → should not be readily replaced
claims.source_ref: Chapter 3: Use the Best Tools

claims.title: Luck does not exist
claims.proposition: Success and failure → follow from → proper methods and causes, not luck
claims.source_ref: Chunk 02, Rothschild maxim and subsequent explanation

claims.title: Perseverance determines success
claims.proposition: Achieving goals → depends on → perseverance through setbacks and doubt
claims.source_ref: Section: Persevere

claims.title: Location determines business success
claims.proposition: Business success → depends on → selecting proper location matching demand
claims.source_ref: Section: Select the Right Location

claims.title: Wholesale effort trumps half measures
claims.proposition: Acquiring fortune → requires → doing business thoroughly with all might
claims.source_ref: Section: Whatever You Do, Do It With All Your Might

claims.title: Integrity is the indispensable foundation of lasting prosperity
claims.proposition: Integrity → functions as → the indispensable foundation of lasting prosperity, because honesty opens credit, dishonesty closes all paths to success, and wealth itself is justified only by responsibility to human happiness
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Preserve Your Integrity
entities: Integrity is more valuable than wealth|integrity surpasses diamonds and rubies
Dishonesty inevitably discovered destroys opportunity|dishonesty is always eventually discovered
Honesty enables community credit access|known honesty
Wealth pursuit requires accepting responsibility|the desire for wealth is laudable
Commerce is civilization's foundation|commerce parallels civilization itself

claims.title: Earned capital builds lasting wealth while unearned capital destroys it
claims.proposition: Self-earned capital → teaches discipline and value understanding that borrowed or inherited capital cannot provide, making earned wealth durable and unearned wealth self-destructive
claims.source_ref: Chapter 4: Don't Get Above Your Business; Chapter 5: Don't Indorse Without Security
entities: Starting with borrowed capital prevents real wealth building|Borrowed capital
Self-earned capital teaches discipline and value|self-earned capital
Poor boys rise more reliably than rich boys|poor boys outperform rich boys
Wealth without earned understanding corrupts character|unearned wealth corrupts character
Easy access to credit encourages dangerous speculation|Easy credit

claims.title: Vocational alignment and practical skill determine business viability
claims.proposition: Success requires → matching natural aptitude to vocation and location, because excellence in a well-chosen field guarantees patronage while misalignment guarantees struggle
claims.source_ref: Sections: Don't Mistake Your Vocation; Select the Right Location; Learn Something Useful; Don't Get Above Your Business
entities: Choose a vocation suited to aptitude|Natural aptitude
Location determines business success|location determines demand
Excellence in one's field cannot fail to succeed|excellence becomes self-rewarding

claims.title: Physical health and sobriety are prerequisites for wealth creation
claims.proposition: Wealth creation → demands → sound health and clear judgment, which tobacco and alcohol systematically destroy
claims.source_ref: Introduction through Chunk 01, health and temperance passages
entities: Good health is foundational to success|Good health

claims.title: Sustained focused effort and perseverance produce wealth, not luck
claims.proposition: Fortune follows → sustained concentration on a single pursuit with full energy, because scattered effort and attribution to luck both prevent the causal accumulation that wealth requires
claims.source_ref: Sections: Persevere; Whatever You Do, Do It With All Your Might; Do Not Scatter Your Powers; Let Hope Predominate
entities: Wholesale effort trumps half measures|Doing business with full might
Constant business changing keeps one poor|jumping between ventures
Focused effort beats scattered powers|undivided attention

claims.title: True economy is disciplined spending that maintains income over expenditure
claims.proposition: True economy → requires → systematic discipline over spending rather than petty frugality, because social pressure and lifestyle creep silently erode the income-expenditure margin
claims.source_ref: Introduction, foundational principle through sofa anecdote
entities: Social comparison drives wasteful spending|social comparison
One purchase triggers cascading expenses|cascading purchases
Economy is not about frugal behavior|True economy
Tracking expenditure reveals hidden luxuries|tracking expenditure

claims.title: Advertising genuine goods creates compounding customer awareness
claims.proposition: Persistent advertising of genuine products → builds → compounding public awareness that converts to purchases, but advertising fraudulent goods accelerates business death
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Advertise Your Business
entities: Quality products can succeed with advertising|A genuine article
Fraudulent goods lose customers permanently|advertising spurious articles

claims.title: Firsthand knowledge and personal oversight protect against business failure
claims.proposition: Business survival → requires → the owner's direct knowledge and oversight, because delegation without expertise invites deception and speculation outside one's competence destroys accumulated wealth
claims.source_ref: Sections: Depend Upon Your Own Personal Exertions; Beware of Outside Operations; Rothschild maxim
entities: Personal oversight exceeds delegation|the employer's eye outweighs a dozen employees' hands
Mistakes are teaching opportunities|mistakes become experience
Speculation outside expertise destroys wealth|venturing outside one's expertise
Caution and boldness both necessary|be cautious in planning and bold in execution

claims.title: Debt enslaves the borrower while enriching the creditor
claims.proposition: Debt → creates → an asymmetric master-servant relationship where compound interest works for the creditor and against the debtor
claims.source_ref: Section: Avoid Debt
entities: Creditors have perfect memories|creditors possess the best memories
Interest compounds for lenders only|money in debt works against the debtor

claims.title: Customer relationships and charitable reputation drive sustained revenue
claims.proposition: Revenue sustains → through politeness that retains customers and generosity that attracts patronage, because each interaction compounds into reputation that either builds or destroys a business
claims.source_ref: Chapter 5: Be Polite and Kind to Your Customers; Be Charitable
entities: Politeness retains customer relationships|Politeness and civility
Customer insult loses future revenue|insulting a paying customer
Targeted charity achieves more good|selective assistance to self-helpers
